title: Only Her Hairdresser Knows for Sure
display_title: Only Her Hairdresser Knows for Sure
wikitext:
[]: Only Her Hairdresser Knows for Sure             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jan 20, 2009 3:46 pm Sidestepping the reality that a cloned descendent can have different hair color from the antecedent... I thought it was interesting to see that Ritsuko at some point changed her hair color to blond. Maybe something to do with distancing herself from her mom, or maybe Gendo wondered if blonds have more fun. More interesting, though, is that apparently Yui/01 exhibits blue-white hair when she "takes her helmet off" at the end of EoE. Yui's hair color there seems rather close to Rei's hair color that we see throughout the series. I wonder if Yui dyed her hair earlier, and now that we in essence just see her soul we now see her natural hair color?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jan 20, 2009 4:40 pm If Yui had a different hair color then brown, then it would be visible in her eyebrows similar to Ritsuko.

[]: Re: Only Her Hairdresser Knows for Sure             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 20, 2009 4:40 pm VoidEater wrote: Yui's hair color there seems rather close to Rei's hair color that we see throughout the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Tue Jan 20, 2009 5:30 pm It looks cool... Besides, Didnt Unit 01 loose most of it's helmet during the first battle, with no sign of hair under it. And no production materials show any hair on the naked 'machine'. I thought it was an eternal flame in memory of the passing of mankind... but that's based on what I thoiught when I saw the thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 21, 2009 3:36 am The script calls it "hair". Not "energy/flame that looks like hair". Just "hair".

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Wed Jan 21, 2009 5:01 am Well, being that she's an armored giant, it's logical that she could've been shaved or something. But of course, after killing the entire human race she decided to go all natural for awhile.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 21, 2009 6:07 am The "shaving" idea feels a bit awkward to me. For what it's worth, both Adam and Lilith would appear to be quite bald; even the wacky forms the Angels take eschew hair! I have, of course, nothing to back this up, but it feels more likely that Eva-01's hair is something that "happens", for whatever bizarre reason, after her massive power-up in EoE. Fans have linked the hair to the phantasmagorical long-haired presence Shinji encounters in episode 16. Shin-seiki (IIRC) suggested that Eva-01 "has become Yui"; this one Japanese page forwards the idea that she's become a goddess of some sort (forget exact details at the moment). I don't think I have any officially-sanctioned materials in my possession that comment on the hair to any effect. So who really knows (besides the usual suspects).

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Jan 21, 2009 9:34 am Rei's blue hair is a dye job, and so is Misato's purple hair. These characters are so vain, it's ridiculous.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 21, 2009 10:45 am Reichu wrote: The script calls it "hair". Not "energy/flame that looks like hair". Just "hair".

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 11:58 am It's hair. Has anyone here seen hair in space? And that amount? I think not.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Jan 21, 2009 12:37 pm @BrikHaus: While hardly anything these post-apocalyptic kids might do would surprise me, I was kinda thinking that Rei's hair might be natural - I wonder if someone as young as we see her would be coloring her hair (Asuka and Shinji seem to have natural coloring to me for the same reasons). The Rei-fish seem to have the same hair color as it might be viewed through LCL; might they have dyed all the hair of the Rei-fish? I hadn't mentioned ep 16 because I couldn't remember which ep that enounter had taken place in - but the form of the hair seems rather similar there, too. It may all be "generic soul representation hair" for all I know, but it was something that piqued my curiosity. There doesn't seem to be a huge amount of randomness in the series (despite inconsistencies).

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Jan 22, 2009 2:28 am The hair shouldn't be waving, cause shes in SPAAAAAAAAACE. </science> not that it matters.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Jan 22, 2009 6:18 am You faggots: Just because the script calls it hair doesn't make it hair. I mean, I've heard several artists, directors, &c. describe something using something it looks similar to. But it probably is actual hair, since Japanese is always literal and the Japanese never make light of anything. Or maybe I'm wrong because I don't agree with Reichu (no disrespect intended, my love ). I guess we'll never know until Ornette can ask Anno for clarification. VoidEater: Yeah, I guess it is pretty rare for anime characters to have unnatural hair colors.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Jan 22, 2009 7:44 am Ha, the truth is here: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zAR8LpQrVtY

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jan 22, 2009 9:42 am ^ Holy crap, that scared the shit out of me.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 22, 2009 1:28 pm The NGE scripts use comparisons pretty prolifically to describe things. "(noun) no you na (noun)", etc. If this wasn't actually hair, I'd expect the same deal to show up. ... expect . Incidentally, there's that scene in NGE2 (which Anno contributed to, blah blah blah) where you can see a silhouette of Eva-01 with long tresses coming out from under the helmet. There's also that frightening Yamashita image of Evas as huge Amazons with long, silver-blue hair and giant knockers. I'd love to think it was just a weird hairy-looking aura or something, since Evas having hair is do not want, but there's this nagging feeling that I'd be wrong...

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Thu Jan 22, 2009 2:16 pm Well, after a massive universe-bending ritual involving Rei (who has blue hair), Lillith (who probably caused Rei to have blue hair), and Yui's soul (born of Lillith, and the person who's DNA gave Rei most of her gorgeous looks aside from the blue hair), it isn't really surprising that the blue hair gets transferred from one to another. Plus, do we really know how long the ritual took once everybody was tanged? Shogoki could have been out there for much longer than we think. I imagine most people don't bother looking at their watch while being tanged and then again after reassembling. She very well might have had time to grow it out. As for the waving, I blame AT field fluctuations and quantum magic. Unit 1's hair waving in space is what caused the universe to spawn life in the first place, don't you know?

[]: Re: Only Her Hairdresser Knows for Sure             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Thu Jan 22, 2009 5:43 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: It's more of an aura/energy than actual hair. And as we know, energy is usually bluish.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Jan 22, 2009 6:35 pm Reichu wrote: but there's this nagging feeling that I'd be wrong...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 24, 2009 5:55 pm Enki v.2 wrote: Lillith (who probably caused Rei to have blue hair)
